channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Afghans fleeing Taliban urge Trump to lift refugee program suspension
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghans who fled after the Taliban seized power appealed Wednesday to U.S. President Donald Trump to exempt them from an order suspending the relocation of refugees to the United States, some saying they risked their lives to support U.S. troops.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Witnesses describe chaos and panic at Turkish ski resort as fire ripped through hotel where 76 died
item.description: KARTALKAYA, Turkey (AP) — As flames tore through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey, friends Esra Karakisa and Halime Cetin watched helpless as the horror unfolded before them, with people leaning out of smoke-filled rooms pleading for help and others making the harrowin
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Long forgotten, Black servicemen who died in WWI are finally honored with a memorial in South Africa
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The names are carved on poles of African hardwood that are set upright as if reaching for the sun. No one knows where the men they represent were buried.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Task Force created in Africa to counter terror fears from Iran and jihadi groups
item.description: South Africa’s Chief Rabbi Dr. Warren Goldstein has created an Anti-Terror Task Force to combat terror from Iran and Jihadi groups globally. The group will also try to protect Christians under attack in Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Davos 2025: Trade, tariffs, AI and UN chief Guterres dominate World Economic Forum agenda
item.description: A speech by the U.N. chief, economic growth potential in places like China and Russia and artificial intelligence have headlined the agenda at the World Economic Forum’s annual event in Davos.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Suspected settlers attacked Palestinian villages hours before Trump rescinded Biden sanctions
item.description: JINSAFUT, West Bank (AP) — Shortly after suspected Jewish settlers stormed Palestinian villages in the occupied West Bank late Monday, setting cars and property ablaze, U.S. President Donald Trump canceled sanctions against Israelis accused of violence in the territory.
item.dc:creator: AREF TUFAHA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Panama, familiar with US intervention, bristles at Trump's comments on canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — President Donald Trump’s insistence Monday that he wants the Panama Canal back under U.S. control fed nationalist sentiment and worry in Panama, home to the critical trade route and a country familiar with U.S. military intervention.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea's Yoon defends his martial law decree in his first public appearance since his arrest
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached president denied Tuesday that he ordered the military to drag lawmakers out of the National Assembly to prevent them from voting to reject his martial law decree last month, as he appeared for the first time before the Constitutional Court that will
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 80 people killed in northeast Colombia as peace talks fail, official says
item.description: More than 80 people were killed in northeast Colombia over the weekend after the government failed to hold peace talks with the National Liberation Army.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Davos 2025 reacts to Trump tack, EU chief leads defense against climate change
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — As the World Economic Forum’s annual gabfest in the Swiss Alps got into full swing on Tuesday, U.S. President Donald Trump gave everybody something to talk about with his actions on his first day back in office.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's top general resigns over Oct. 7 failures, adding to pressure on Netanyahu
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's top general resigned Tuesday, taking responsibility for security failures tied to Hamas' surprise attack that triggered the war in Gaza and adding to pressure on Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who has delayed any public inquiry that could potentially implicate his leade
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel launches a major West Bank operation, days after Gaza ceasefire
item.description: Israel launched a major military operation Tuesday in the occupied West Bank city of Jenin that killed at least nine Palestinians and left at least 40 more people wounded, Palestinian health officials said, as Israel’s fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza entered its third day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin, Xi vow to ‘deepen’ alliance hours after Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin held a video call hours after President Donald Trump re-entered the White House and pledged to "deepen" their partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico defends sovereignty as US seeks to label cartels as terrorists
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Tuesday that President Donald Trump's executive order moving toward designating drug cartels as foreign terrorist organizations would only impact Mexico if there's close coordination between the two governments.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russia warned President Trump on Tuesday against seizing the Panama Canal, after he reiterated his intent to take control of the strategic waterway in his second inaugural address Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump: Putin 'destroying Russia' with war in Ukraine
item.description: President Trump said Russian President Vladimir Putin is “destroying Russia” with its nearly three-year war in Ukraine, adding he hopes the Kremlin leader is open to making a deal to end the conflict. "He should make a deal.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: World leaders react as Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Leaders across the globe reacted to Donald Trump once again entering the White House, with some nations issuing messages of congratulations while others took issue with his inaugural comments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli military launches large-scale counterterrorism operation in West Bank city
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces announced an operation in the West Bank city of Jenin, which is a stronghold for terror organizations backed by Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China's population falls for a third straight year, posing challenges for its government and economy
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China's population fell last year for the third straight year, its government said Friday, pointing to further demographic challenges for the world's second most populous nation, which is now facing both an aging population and an emerging shortage of working age people.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yemen's Houthis signal they'll now limit their attacks in the Red Sea corridor to Israeli ships
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels signaled Monday they now will limit their attacks in the Red Sea corridor to only Israeli-affiliated ships after a ceasefire began in the Gaza Strip, but warned wider assaults could resume if needed.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: The Senate on Monday unanimously confirmed Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) as America's 72nd secretary of State, putting in place the first member of President Trump's Cabinet on the day of his inauguration. Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israeli president calls Trump a 'true friend' on Inauguration Day, praises his work to release hostages
item.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog is praising Donald Trump as a "true friend" of Israel, thanking him for his "commitment to bringing all our hostages home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel Defense Forces will receive hostages Sunday with equipped camper trailers and comforting supplies
item.description: The Israel Defense forces completed preparations Saturday to receive 33 hostages being held by Hama Sunday after a cease-fire deal was reached.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gunman shoots dead 2 Supreme Court judges in Iran's capital before turning gun on himself, state media says
item.description: Two Iranian Supreme Court judges were shot and killed in Tehran on Saturday while a third judge and a bodyguard were wounded, although the motive is unclear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hurts his right arm after falling for the second time in just over a month
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis fell Thursday and hurt his right arm, the Vatican said, just weeks after another apparent fall resulted in a bad bruise on his chin. Francis didn’t break his arm, but a sling was put on as a precaution, the Vatican spokesman said in a statement On Dec.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation
